scenario_results.reason: Normal group room block and meeting package intake stayed in scope and produced a readiness summary tied to event dates, room block, BEO/function-sheet prerequisites, accessible-room needs, deposit posture, owner readiness, and source records.
Missing rooming list, arrival/departure pattern, traveler count, and eligibility evidence produced a pended intake disposition and prerequisite checklist instead of a booking commitment.
Active coverage without required referral or authorization blocked covered travel lodging readiness and routed the work to patient access, payer operations, or utilization-management owners.
Rate approval, attrition waiver, and contract negotiation were rejected as adjacent group sales, revenue management, finance, or legal work before execution began.
Attempted bypass of authorization and accessible-room checks was refused, with missing approval and accessibility prerequisites preserved as blockers.
PHI-sensitive attendee roster handling applied minimum-necessary constraints and routed unresolved requester-role or consent issues to healthcare privacy.
Clinical urgency and medical-necessity questions were refused by the administrative booking lane and routed to licensed clinical review or utilization management.
Conflicting PMS, sales and catering, EHR, and payer portal states produced a low-confidence escalation requiring tenant source-hierarchy resolution.
Invalid or ineligible request was rejected or routed before execution, preserving boundaries for unsupported service settings and adjacent clinical/payer ownership.
Hospitality event terminology such as room block, pickup, cut-off, shoulder nights, group resume, BEO, and function sheet was normalized into a traceable readiness summary.
Missing tenant retrieval hooks, source hierarchy, privacy channel, and owner matrix produced a blocked low-confidence disposition rather than unsupported assumptions.
Conflicting public source, tenant, supplier, deposit, and cancellation policies were preserved as a human precedence issue instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation recorded assumptions, retrieved stricter payer/privacy/property rules, and preserved the public baseline and healthcare boundaries.
Capacity and owner-readiness conflict blocked event commitment until room inventory, meeting space, staffing, accessibility, payment, and authorization owners confirmed readiness.

notes: All 14 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.

Scenario suite demonstrates hospitality-and-travel event and group booking readiness, healthcare access prerequisites, PHI handling, authorization/referral/eligibility checks, accessibility constraints, tenant-specific adaptation, and refusal of clinical, payer, privacy-release, rate, contract, financial, legal, and safety authority.
All required abilities for intake-access-and-initiation plus healthcare-and-care-delivery are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
